name: Covalagua and Páramo de la Lora Loop
name_original: Miradores de Lora y de Valcabado — circular por el Paisaje Protegido Covalagua
description: This difficult 61.3-mile road cycling route offers panoramic views and karst landscapes in the Covalagua Protected Landscape.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Basconcillos Del Tozo traverse a diverse landscape within Spain's Castile and León region, characterized by rolling hills and more challenging terrain. The area is part of the Páramos comarca and lies within the Las Loras Unesco World Geopark, offering routes through limestone formations and river valleys. Cyclists can expect a peaceful countryside experience with varied elevation profiles, often on mostly paved surfaces with light motor vehicle traffic. This region provides a blend of physical challenge and natural beauty for road cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Basconcillos Del Tozo The most popular road cycling route is Hoces del Alto Ebro y Rudrón Park Loop, a 32.2 miles (51.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 27 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the natural park with its river gorges. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Covalagua and Páramo de la Lora Loop, a difficult 61.3 miles (98.7 km) path. This route features the Covalagua Natural Area and the high plateau of Páramo de la Lora. Local road cyclists also love the Aurora Fountain – Puente de Piedra de Polientes loop from Polientes, a 64.9 miles (104.4 km) trail leading through scenic landscapes and past local landmarks, often completed in about 4 hours 31 minutes. Road cycling around Basconcillos Del Tozo is defined by rolling hills, limestone formations, and river valleys. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Basconcillos Del Tozo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from a few reviews. More than 170 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Basconcillos Del Tozo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Basconcillos Del Tozo?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes around Basconcillos Del Tozo, offering a diverse range of experiences for cyclists. These routes traverse varied landscapes, including rolling hills, limestone formations, and river valleys.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Basconcillos Del Tozo?
A: Road cycling routes in Basconcillos Del Tozo feature a mix of rolling hills and more challenging terrain, with varied elevation profiles. The area is part of the Las Loras Unesco World Geopark, so you can expect routes through geologically rich areas, including limestone formations and river valleys. Most surfaces are paved, often with light motor vehicle traffic, providing a peaceful countryside experience.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, several routes are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005123478/hoces-del-alto-ebro-y-rudron-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoces del Alto Ebro y Rudrón Park Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 32.2-mile route exploring the natural park, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058301666/covalagua-and-paramo-de-la-lora-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covalagua and Páramo de la Lora Loop&lt;/a&gt; offers a more difficult 61.3-mile challenge through the Covalagua Natural Area.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer access to stunning natural features. You can cycle near the strikingly blue spring waters of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2577310" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo Azul de Covanera&lt;/a&gt;, or visit the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3066377" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cañada Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20159527" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cañada Waterfall – Sotresgudo loop from Hoyos del Tozo&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route that passes by this waterfall. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3932476" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valcabado Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; offers spectacular panoramic views of the Valderredible Valley.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling options in Basconcillos Del Tozo?
A: While specific family-friendly routes are not highlighted, the region offers routes with varying difficulty levels, including easy and moderate options. Many local roads have light motor vehicle traffic, which can make for a more relaxed cycling experience. It's advisable to check the elevation and distance of individual routes to ensure they suit your family's capabilities.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Basconcillos Del Tozo?
A: The region's elevation of 917 meters (approximately 3,008 feet) above sea level contributes to scenic views and fresh air, making it conducive to outdoor activities. Generally, spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures for cycling, avoiding the peak heat of summer or the colder conditions of winter. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Basconcillos Del Tozo?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the peaceful countryside experience, the diverse terrain, and the opportunities to explore natural parks and geological wonders away from busy main roads.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Basconcillos Del Tozo offers several difficult routes for experienced cyclists. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058301666/covalagua-and-paramo-de-la-lora-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covalagua and Páramo de la Lora Loop&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, is a challenging 61.3-mile path with significant elevation gain, featuring the high plateau of Páramo de la Lora. Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20159527" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cañada Waterfall – Sotresgudo loop from Hoyos del Tozo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find cafes or pubs along the road cycling routes?
A: While specific cafes or pubs are not detailed for every route, the region offers a peaceful countryside experience. It's recommended to plan your route in advance and check for villages or towns along your chosen path where you might find local establishments for refreshments. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31493081" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aurora Fountain – Puente de Piedra de Polientes loop from Polientes&lt;/a&gt;, for example, passes through Polientes, which may offer such amenities.

Q: What are the typical elevation gains on road cycling routes in this region?
A: Elevation gains vary significantly across routes, reflecting the diverse terrain. For example, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005123478/hoces-del-alto-ebro-y-rudron-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoces del Alto Ebro y Rudrón Park Loop&lt;/a&gt; has an elevation gain of approximately 650 meters, while the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058301666/covalagua-and-paramo-de-la-lora-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covalagua and Páramo de la Lora Loop&lt;/a&gt; features over 1100 meters of ascent. This range allows cyclists to choose routes that match their desired challenge level.

Q: Are there any waterfalls accessible by road bike?
A: Yes, the region is home to beautiful waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3066377" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cañada Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a notable natural feature that can be incorporated into a ride, as seen in the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20159527" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cañada Waterfall – Sotresgudo loop from Hoyos del Tozo&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5619942" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tubilla del Agua Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; are another scenic spot you might encounter on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24567872" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tubilla del Agua Waterfalls – Hermitage of San Roque loop from Barrio Panizares&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the general road surface quality for cycling in Basconcillos Del Tozo?
A: Cyclists can anticipate mostly paved surfaces on many of the routes. The local roads often have light motor vehicle traffic, contributing to a more pleasant and relaxed cycling experience away from busier main roads.
tips: Right next to the OAC (Observatorio Astronómico de Cantabria) is the viewpoint of Lora that offers impressive views of the Valle de Valderredible, a valley that stretches meters beyond Río Ebro
Stone bridge that crosses the Ebro river.
Town of origin road on the road from Quintanilla de las Torres to Villanueva de la Nía, which is 9 km away. Its town center is divided by the road, which crosses it.
This viewpoint is located in a privileged location, on a spectacular natural balcony over the “Monte Ahedo” beech forest, dominating the Valderredible Valley.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 98.7 km (61.3 miles) long with over 1,100 meters (3,600 feet) of elevation gain. Komoot estimates the cycling duration to be around 4 hours and 30 minutes, but this can vary based on your fitness level, stops for sightseeing, and road conditions.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for road cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: The Covalagua Protected Landscape features elevated limestone surfaces and varied terrain. While the broader region offers well-maintained roads, some segments of this circular route, especially those connecting viewpoints, may include unpaved paths, gravel, or rougher surfaces that could be challenging for a typical road bicycle. Be prepared for varied conditions and consider a bike with more robust tires if you're concerned about off-pavement sections.

faqs.question: What are the main sights and viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers spectacular panoramic views, particularly from the Mirador de Lora and the Valcabado Viewpoint. From Valcabado, you can see the Valderredible Valley and up to forty villages on clear days. Other notable points include the Puente de Piedra de Polientes and the settlement of San Martín de Valdelomar.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating on Komoot, the significant distance (nearly 100 km), and over 1,100 meters of elevation gain, this route is not recommended for beginners. It requires good physical fitness and experience with challenging climbs and descents.

faqs.question: What wildlife and plants can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The Covalagua Protected Landscape is rich in biodiversity. You might spot deer, wild boars, foxes, and various birds of prey like the golden eagle. The landscape features mixed forests of beech, oak, and pine, showcasing a blend of Atlantic and Mediterranean vegetation.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is generally during the spring or autumn months. These seasons offer pleasant temperatures, vibrant natural scenery, and typically more stable weather conditions. Summer can be hot, and winter may bring colder temperatures and potential for snow or ice at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for the Covalagua Protected Landscape or Las Loras Geopark?
faqs.answer: No, generally you do not need a specific permit or pay an entrance fee to cycle within the Covalagua Protected Landscape or the broader Las Loras UNESCO Global Geopark. These areas are open for public enjoyment, though specific regulations for protected areas should always be respected.

faqs.question: Is this route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed in natural parks in Spain, specific regulations may apply within the Covalagua Protected Landscape. It's always best to keep dogs on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure their safety, especially given the presence of wild animals and potential for varied terrain.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the route?
faqs.answer: Specific parking areas for this exact circular route are not detailed, but you can typically find parking in villages within or near the Covalagua Protected Landscape, such as Revilla de Pomar or Pomar de Valdivia. Look for designated public parking spots in these areas to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can find cafes, pubs, and accommodation options in the villages surrounding the Covalagua Protected Landscape, such as Revilla de Pomar, Pomar de Valdivia, or other nearby towns in the Palencia region. These can be good spots to refuel or stay overnight.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route briefly intersect with other paths. You may encounter parts of the Camino natural de Ebro - Cantabria, Sendero del Petróleo, and Camino de San Andres a carretera de Helecha, among others.

waypoints.name: View of the Valderredible Valley from Mirador de Lora
